content: agent1: working on extracting normalize_image_bytes helper in llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py (refactoring image_to_base64). Will touch only that file.

agent1: my diff is small and self-contained — adds module-level normalize_image_bytes(image) -> (encoded_image, decoded_for_guessing) above MessageRole class, and image_to_base64 now does: encoded_image, decoded_for_guessing = normalize_image_bytes(self.image); if not self.image_mimetype: guess = filetype.guess(decoded_for_guessing)...; self.image = encoded_image. This only touches the b64-encoding/decoding decision, not where mimetype guessing itself lives. Your get_image_mimetype() lazy accessor sounds orthogonal — you could have image_to_base64 call normalize_image_bytes for the encoded_image/decoded_for_guessing tuple, then pass decoded_for_guessing into your get_image_mimetype() (or cache it there) instead of calling filetype.guess directly. I'll finish and submit my patch now since it's minimal; feel free to layer your lazy-accessor changes on top in your own patch.txt (this is submitted independently per-agent, so no need to literally merge in this working tree).
